items.title: Noted on pod but my broadest take on the Devin Carter trade is...
items.author: Brad Rowland
items.body_excerpt:
Noted on pod but my broadest take on the Devin Carter trade is...

If the Hawks had traded a future asset for Carter, I would've been "meh" on the deal, even while totally understanding it.

Instead, the Hawks RECEIVED a future asset to take Carter, pushing it to a "clear yes" for me.

items.title: Without altering any of the projections, @stephnoh.bsky.social's contract model has Devin Carter projected at $5.8 million for the 2026-27 season.
items.author: Brad Rowland
items.body_excerpt:
Without altering any of the projections, @stephnoh.bsky.social's contract model has Devin Carter projected at $5.8 million for the 2026-27 season.

If you raise up his games played (he was at 38 last year) to even 60 games, it jumps to about $9 million.

Model here: nbasalarymodel.xyz

items.title: Hawks cap situation after declining Jonathan Kuminga and trading for Devin Carter.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
Hawks cap situation after declining Jonathan Kuminga and trading for Devin Carter.

They have plenty of flexibility now to use the mid-level exception or make additional trades, like for a big man.

Carter can go into a trade exception or as part of a bigger Buddy Hield trade.

items.title: The Sacramento Kings are trading Devin Carter and a 2033 second-round pick to the Atlanta Hawks, league sources told HoopsHype. Carter is owed $5.16 million next season, which now comes off the books for Sacramento next season.
